hero.title: Custom Lightweight Minecraft Server In
hero.desc: RustMC is a custom, highly modular, thread-safe Minecraft server built from the ground up in Rust. Designed to support Minecraft 26.1.2 (Protocol 775) status querying, handshakes, login compression, configuration syncing, and flat world multi-player sandbox interaction.

tokio.title: Tokio Concurrency
tokio.desc: Async multi-threading loops handle hundreds of concurrent player handshakes, tick cycles, and chunk streaming without frame drops.

config.title: Dynamic YAML Configuration
config.desc: Fully decoupled configuration parsing (similar to Paper/Spigot settings) dynamically maps MOTD, game mode, max players, pvp, flight flags, and limits.

transfer.title: Cross-Server Transfers
transfer.desc: Implements secure Cookie Transfer negotiation to authenticate and hand off player sessions between federated servers smoothly.

quickstart.title: Quick Start Guide
quickstart.desc: Build and run the RustMC server binary locally. Follow the steps below:
quickstart.build: # Build the workspace in release mode
quickstart.run: # Run the server. It will auto-create a default server.yaml configuration

architecture.title: Core Server Architecture
architecture.desc: RustMC implements a highly concurrent network handler structured around Tokio tasks, actor channels, and shared synchronized memory regions.

splitting.title: Connection Reader & Writer Splitting
splitting.desc: Upon accepting a TCP client socket, RustMC splits the stream into a <0>Reader</0> loop and a <1>Writer</1> loop. This eliminates read/write thread contention. The Reader loop decodes packets, processes clientbound commands, and communicates state changes to a centralized loop via Tokio channels, while the Writer actor flushes outbound packet buffers.

state.title: In-Memory Shared World State
state.desc: World layouts, player metadata registries, and blocks configuration are managed in memory, wrapped in thread-safe shared references: <0>Arc<RwLock<WorldState>></0>. Multiple reader threads can retrieve player position changes simultaneously, while write locks are only requested during tick updates, ensuring minimal mutex contention.

broadcasting.title: Event Broadcasting
broadcasting.desc: The server utilizes a centralized <0>tokio::sync::broadcast</0> channel to propagate text chat, joint alerts, server notices, and player movement synchronization updates to all connection loops.
broadcasting.step_3: Awaits handshake packet to parse next stage (Status or Login).
broadcasting.step_5: Transitions client connection into Play phase.
broadcasting.step_6: Subscribes to global server broadcast channel.

world.title: World Generation & Chunk Persistence
world.desc: RustMC implements a high-performance world orchestration system that handles custom world generation, dynamic spawn height detection, asynchronous chunk serialization via Zlib compression, and robust automated backups.

generators.title: World Generators
generators.desc: The server supports two world generation styles, configurable via the <0>world_type</0> parameter in <1>server.yaml</1>:
generators.flat: <0>Flat World (<1>flat</1>):</0> Generates a uniform layered terrain consisting of 1 Bedrock, 2 Dirt, and 1 Grass block at the configured <2>sea_level</2> (default Y=63). Ideal for testing and lightweight lobby setups.
generators.normal: <0>Normal World (<1>normal</1>):</0> Utilizes a noise generator based on a pseudo-random number seed (specified by <2>seed</2>) to produce natural terrain contours, hills, and valleys that emulate standard Minecraft environments.

spawn.title: Spawn Height Detection
spawn.desc: To prevent players from suffocating or falling endlessly into the void when spawning, RustMC implements an automated <0>Spawn Height Finder</0>:
spawn.step_2: Scans downward from the sky limit (Y=319) at the target (X, Z).
spawn.step_3: Locates the first non-air block state.
spawn.step_4: Ensures the block is solid (not air or fluid).
spawn.step_5: Sets spawn position precisely on top of this block.

persistence.title: Chunk Serialization & Compression
persistence.desc: World chunks are persisted to disk inside the directory configured by <0>world_dir</0>. Each chunk is saved in its own file using a compressed JSON strategy to maximize space efficiency:
persistence.meta_desc: Saves world-wide attributes including spawn coordinates (X, Y, Z), world seed, world type, difficulty, and sea level.
persistence.chunk_desc: Individual chunk columns are serialized to JSON structure containing block state palettes, non-air block counts, and block arrays, then compressed using Zlib.
persistence.io_desc: All reading and writing of compressed chunk data is performed using Rust's file system handles, ensuring integrity and speed.

tasks.title: Automated Background Tasks & Backups
tasks.desc: RustMC manages persistence operations in the background using lightweight asynchronous Tokio loops, keeping the server main tick thread responsive:
tasks.autosave_desc: Runs in a background task at the frequency set by <0>save_interval_secs</0> (default: 300s). The loop acquires a read lock on the world state, identifies modified/dirty chunks, and serializes them asynchronously to avoid interrupting connection processing.
tasks.backup_desc: Runs at the frequency set by <0>backup_interval_secs</0> (default: 3600s). The task creates a full copy of the world directory under <1>backups/backup_<millisecond_timestamp>/</1>. To prevent blocking the async runtime, I/O copying is offloaded to a thread pool via <2>tokio::task::spawn_blocking</2>.
tasks.prune_desc: To prevent the storage volume from filling up over time, the backup system enforces a pruning algorithm: after a successful backup, it scans the backups directory, sorts backups chronologically, and deletes the oldest folders so that only the latest <0>max_backups</0> (default: 5) are kept.

protocol_tab.title: Minecraft Protocol Flow
protocol_tab.desc: Connections traverse a sequential multi-state workflow defined by the Minecraft networking protocol specification.

transfer_tab.title: Cross-Server Cookie Transfer Protocol
transfer_tab.desc: RustMC implements the modern Minecraft cookie transfer and redirection handshake to allow seamless hub-to-spoke network redirects.

steps.negotiate_desc: The source server sends a Transfer packet to the client, supplying the destination server hostname and port along with unique routing parameters.
steps.store_desc: Prior to the client transitioning, the source server posts a cryptographic verification token (cookie token) to the destination server's auth cache.
steps.handoff_desc: Upon connecting to the destination server, the client transmits the verification token during the login sequence. The destination verifies it to restore state.

flow_card.title: Cookie Validation Protocol Flow
flow_card.desc: The protocol secures transfers against session hijacking using HMAC signed request payloads exchanged between federated instances.
flow_card.comment: // Pseudo-code logic for verification on destination server:

security_tab.title: Security & Operator Permissions
security_tab.desc: RustMC implements rate limit protections on client streams and maps administration roles dynamically via the Operators system.

rate_limiting.title: Connection Rate Limiting
rate_limiting.desc: To prevent denial of service (DoS) and spamming, connection actors monitor inbound packets. If a player exceeds the configured <0>invalid_packet_threshold</0> within the window specified by <1>invalid_packet_window_secs</1>, the connection is instantly severed and blacklisted for the epoch.

ops.title: Operators System (ops.toml)
ops.desc: Server operators are configured inside <0>ops.toml</0> using their username, UUID, and integer permission levels (1-4). On connection login, the server matches credentials and streams the operator rank down to the client to activate server commands.

packet_ref.title: Minecraft Protocol Packets
packet_ref.desc: Interactive guide to Minecraft 26.1.2 (Protocol 775) packet framing implemented in RustMC.
packet_ref.empty_payload: Empty payload or dynamic data structure
packet_ref.no_matches: No packets match the filter criteria.

config_gen.title: Interactive Configuration Generator
config_gen.desc: Customize your RustMC settings using standard values, and instantly generate a matching `server.yaml` file.

server.title: server
server.description: Core networking and server allocation configurations.

rate_limit.title: rate_limit
rate_limit.description: Connection rate limits and DDoS flood protection mechanisms.

gameplay.title: gameplay
gameplay.description: Minecraft mechanics, world parameters, and rule enforcements.

bind.name: Bind Address
bind.description: The IP address and port that the RustMC server binds to.

view_distance.name: View Distance
view_distance.description: The maximum chunk distance the server will stream to players.

invalid_packet_threshold.name: Invalid Packet Threshold
invalid_packet_threshold.description: Maximum number of malformed or invalid packets allowed before dropping a connection.

invalid_packet_window_secs.name: Window Duration (secs)
invalid_packet_window_secs.description: The sliding time window in seconds during which invalid packets are accumulated.

motd.name: Message of the Day (MOTD)
motd.description: The description of the server displayed in the client server list.

max_players.name: Max Players
max_players.description: The maximum concurrent players allowed to join the server.

gamemode.name: Game Mode
gamemode.description: Initial game mode assigned to new players joining the world.

difficulty.name: Difficulty
difficulty.description: Toggles structural threat levels and damage scales.

pvp.name: PVP Enabled
pvp.description: Allows players to deal combat damage to each other.

allow_flight.name: Allow Flight
allow_flight.description: Enables flight capabilities in survival modes, bypassing anti-cheat flags.

hardcore.name: Hardcore Mode
hardcore.description: If active, players are permanently put in spectator mode upon dying.

simulation_distance.name: Simulation Distance
simulation_distance.description: Ticking range of chunks surrounding the player.

sea_level.name: Sea Level
sea_level.description: Base sea height level utilized during flat chunk generation.

world_type.name: World Type
world_type.description: Determines the world generation style. "flat" generates a flat grass plain, "normal" generates a noise-based surface.

seed.name: World Seed
seed.description: Seed for random number generator utilized during normal noise terrain generation.

world_dir.name: World Directory
world_dir.description: Directory path where the world state (chunks, level info) is saved and loaded.

save_interval_secs.name: Save Interval (secs)
save_interval_secs.description: Time interval in seconds between automatic background saving of modified chunks.

backup_interval_secs.name: Backup Interval (secs)
backup_interval_secs.description: Time interval in seconds between automatic background backups of the world directory.

max_backups.name: Max Backups
max_backups.description: Maximum number of automated backups to keep before older backups are pruned.

Status Request.name: Status Request
Status Request.description: Sent by the client immediately after handshaking to request server status metadata.

Status Response.name: Status Response
Status Response.description: Returns a JSON structure detailing the server version, maximum and current players, and MOTD.

fields.JSON Response: Serialized JSON string conforming to Minecraft status format.

Ping Request.name: Ping Request
Ping Request.description: Sent by the client to measure latency. Contains an arbitrary payload.

Pong Response.name: Pong Response
Pong Response.description: Sent by the server to respond to a Ping Request, echoing back the same payload.

fields.Payload: 64-bit integer timestamp corresponding to the Ping Request.

Hello (Login Start).name: Hello (Login Start)
Hello (Login Start).description: Initiates the login flow. Specifies username and player UUID.

Login Compression.name: Login Compression
Login Compression.description: Sent by the server to enable compression for all subsequent packet communications.

fields.Threshold: Packet size threshold (in bytes) above which packets are zlib-compressed.

Login Finished.name: Login Finished
Login Finished.description: Confirms successful authentication and transition out of the login phase.

Login Acknowledged.name: Login Acknowledged
Login Acknowledged.description: Acknowledges receipt of Login Finished and transitions client state to Configuration.

Select Known Packs.name: Select Known Packs
Select Known Packs.description: Server sends list of known data/resource packs for the client to confirm sync state.

Select Known Packs Response.name: Select Known Packs Response
Select Known Packs Response.description: Client responds with the subset of known packs that it actually has cached.

Registry Data.name: Registry Data
Registry Data.description: Transfers dimension types, biomes, chat types, and damage types registries in NBT formats.

fields.Registry ID: The namespace ID of the registry (e.g. minecraft:dimension_type).
fields.Entries Count: Number of elements in this registry.

Update Tags.name: Update Tags
Update Tags.description: Sends block, item, fluid, and entity tags configuration mappings to the client.

Finish Configuration.name: Finish Configuration
Finish Configuration.description: Sent by the server to signal completion of the configuration sync handshake.

Acknowledge Finish Configuration.name: Acknowledge Finish Configuration
Acknowledge Finish Configuration.description: Client acknowledges configuration completion and transitions the connection state to Play.

Login (Play).name: Login (Play)
Login (Play).description: Initial state configuration packet entering the active gameplay world. Transmits world physics settings.

fields.Hardcore: Whether the server is in hardcore mode.
fields.Game Mode: The player's initial gamemode (Survival: 0, Creative: 1, etc.).

Set Entity Metadata.name: Set Entity Metadata
Set Entity Metadata.description: Updates entity characteristics, such as visibility, equipment, or active pose.

Player Position.name: Player Position
Player Position.description: Synchronizes or forces the client player's position and rotation coordinates.

Level Chunk With Light.name: Level Chunk With Light
Level Chunk With Light.description: Transmits world voxel blocks, section structures, and lighting grids to the client.

fields.Chunk X / Z: Absolute grid coordinates of the loaded chunk.

Chunk Batch Start.name: Chunk Batch Start
Chunk Batch Start.description: Signals the start of a multi-chunk loading sequence, allowing the client to batch rendering.

Chunk Batch Finished.name: Chunk Batch Finished
Chunk Batch Finished.description: Signals the termination of the current chunk loading sequence batch.

fields.Batch Size: Number of chunks transferred in this batch.

Chunk Batch Received.name: Chunk Batch Received
Chunk Batch Received.description: Sent by the client to confirm rendering and receipt of the chunk batch, throttling network throughput.

Client Tick End.name: Client Tick End
Client Tick End.description: Sent by the client at the end of each local tick loop to trigger server update cycles.

Player Loaded.name: Player Loaded
Player Loaded.description: Client signals that the local player has finished loading terrain and is ready for ticks.

System Chat.name: System Chat
System Chat.description: Transmits text logs or system notices to the client chat UI.

fields.Message: Chat message formatted in Minecraft JSON Text component format.

Keep Alive (Clientbound).name: Keep Alive (Clientbound)
Keep Alive (Clientbound).description: Periodic server heartbeat to ensure connection viability.

Keep Alive (Serverbound).name: Keep Alive (Serverbound)
Keep Alive (Serverbound).description: Heartbeat echo response from client validating connection vitality.
